text:
While the self-attention mechanism has revolutionized natural language processing, its transition to computer vision has encountered several structural obstacles. Traditional self-attention often overlooks the intrinsic 2D nature of images by treating them as 1D sequences, incurs prohibitive quadratic computational costs at high resolutions, and focuses exclusively on spatial relationships while neglecting the importance of channel-wise adaptability. 

In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention mechanism designed to overcome these limitations. By strategically decomposing large-kernel convolutions, LKA effectively captures long-range correlations and local contextual information while maintaining high efficiency and enabling both spatial and channel adaptability. Building on this module, we present the Visual Attention Network (VAN), a simple yet highly effective hierarchical backbone. 

Extensive experimental results demonstrate that VAN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of tasks. For instance, VAN-B6 achieves an impressive 87.8% Top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and establishes a new state-of-the-art performance of 58.2% PQ for panoptic segmentation on the COCO dataset. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly surpasses the Swin Transformer in semantic segmentation on ADE20K and object detection on COCO, offering a powerful and lightweight baseline for the computer vision community.
